content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/migrationpolicies/list/components/MigrationPolicyCreateForm/copmonents/MigrationPolicyFormFooter/MigrationPolicyFormFooter.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 77, 83
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 77):
   76                onClick={handleSubmit}
   77                variant={ButtonVariant.primary}
   78              >

  (line 83):
   82            <ActionListItem>
   83              <Button onClick={closeModal} variant={ButtonVariant.link}>
   84                {t('Cancel')}

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 73, 83
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: ActionListItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 73):
   72            <ActionListItem>
   73              <Button
   74                isDisabled={isSubmitting}

  (line 83):
   82            <ActionListItem>
   83              <Button onClick={closeModal} variant={ButtonVariant.link}>
   84                {t('Cancel')}

```

### Fix 3
Lines: 55, 58, 60, 70
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component]:
Component <StackItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <StackItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <StackItem>{content} other children</StackItem>
  After:  <StackItem component=>other children</StackItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: StackItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Stack

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: StackItem
Prop: component
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 55):
   54        {error && (
   55          <StackItem>
   56            <Alert isInline title={t('An error occurred')} variant={AlertVariant.danger}>

  (line 58):
   57              <Stack hasGutter>
   58                <StackItem>{error?.message}</StackItem>
   59                {error?.href && (

  (line 60):
   59                {error?.href && (
   60                  <StackItem>
   61                    <a href={error.href} rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">

  (line 70):
   69        )}
   70        <StackItem>
   71          <ActionList>

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/migrationpolicies/list/components/MigrationPolicyCreateForm/copmonents/MigrationPolicyFormFooter/MigrationPolicyFormFooter.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll start by analyzing each fix, then read the file to apply them.

**Fix 1**: `Button.variant` type changed - but the values used (`ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.link`) are still valid in the new type. No code change needed.

**Fix 2**: Icon children should be moved to `icon` prop on `<Button>`. I need to check if the Buttons contain icon elements.

**Fix 3**: `StackItem` has a new `component` prop. I need to check if any children should be moved to it. The `component` prop in PatternFly v6's `StackItem` is actually for specifying the HTML element type (like `div`, `section`, etc.), not for absorbing JSX children. This is a false positive for content migration.

Let me read the file first:

Now let me analyze each fix against the actual code:

**Fix 1** (`Button.variant` type changed): Lines 77 and 83 use `ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.link`. Both `'primary'` and `'link'` exist in both the old and new type unions. The only change is the addition of `'stateful'`. **No code change needed** — the existing values are still valid.

**Fix 2** (icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`): Looking at the two Button usages:
- Line 73-80: `<Button ...>{t('Create')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 83-85: `<Button onClick={closeModal} variant={ButtonVariant.link}>{t('Cancel')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**

**Fix 3** (`StackItem` new `component` prop): Looking at each StackItem:
- Line 55: Contains an `<Alert>` — this is content, not an HTML element type. **No change needed.**
- Line 58: Contains `{error?.message}` — text content. **No change needed.**
- Line 60: Contains an `<a>` link — content. **No change needed.**
- Line 70: Contains `<ActionList>` — content. **No change needed.**

The `component` prop on `StackItem` in PF v6 is for specifying the rendered HTML element (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not for absorbing JSX children. All the `StackItem` usages here are passing content as children correctly.

All three fixes require no actual code changes — the existing code is already compatible with PatternFly v6.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): **No change needed.** The values `ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.link` used at lines 77 and 83 are still valid in the new type union. The only difference is the addition of `'stateful'`, which doesn't affect existing code.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to `icon` prop on Button): **No change needed.** Neither Button instance contains icon elements — both contain only text children (`{t('Create')}` and `{t('Cancel')}`). The rule only applies when `<Button>` has icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons` as children.

3. **Fix 3** (StackItem new `component` prop): **No change needed.** The `component` prop on `StackItem` is for specifying the HTML element type (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not for absorbing JSX content children. All four `StackItem` usages correctly pass their content as children.

Now let me analyze each fix against the actual code:

**Fix 1** (`Button.variant` type changed): Lines 77 and 83 use `ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.link`. Both `'primary'` and `'link'` exist in both the old and new type unions. The only change is the addition of `'stateful'`. **No code change needed** — the existing values are still valid.

**Fix 2** (icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`): Looking at the two Button usages:
- Line 73-80: `<Button ...>{t('Create')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 83-85: `<Button onClick={closeModal} variant={ButtonVariant.link}>{t('Cancel')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**

**Fix 3** (`StackItem` new `component` prop): Looking at each StackItem:
- Line 55: Contains an `<Alert>` — this is content, not an HTML element type. **No change needed.**
- Line 58: Contains `{error?.message}` — text content. **No change needed.**
- Line 60: Contains an `<a>` link — content. **No change needed.**
- Line 70: Contains `<ActionList>` — content. **No change needed.**

The `component` prop on `StackItem` in PF v6 is for specifying the rendered HTML element (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not for absorbing JSX children. All the `StackItem` usages here are passing content as children correctly.

All three fixes require no actual code changes — the existing code is already compatible with PatternFly v6.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): **No change needed.** The values `ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.link` used at lines 77 and 83 are still valid in the new type union. The only difference is the addition of `'stateful'`, which doesn't affect existing code.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to `icon` prop on Button): **No change needed.** Neither Button instance contains icon elements — both contain only text children (`{t('Create')}` and `{t('Cancel')}`). The rule only applies when `<Button>` has icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons` as children.

3. **Fix 3** (StackItem new `component` prop): **No change needed.** The `component` prop on `StackItem` is for specifying the HTML element type (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not for absorbing JSX content children. All four `StackItem` usages correctly pass their content as children.
